evals.prompt: Use NVFLARE Auto-FL to optimize ./job.py for validation accuracy in simulation with an 8-candidate budget.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes the NVFLARE Auto-FL skill, imports job.py into autofl.yaml, summarizes editable/unresolved/allowed campaign settings, then prepares and evaluates agent-authored source or tunable candidates through candidate manifests and existing FLARE execution surfaces.
assertions: The agent generates autofl.yaml before editing files.
The agent summarizes editable settings, unresolved fields, fixed-budget constraints, and allowed edit paths.
The agent forms hypotheses and edits isolated candidate source rather than limiting the campaign to built-in tunable sweeps.
The agent records changed files, patch hash, base candidate, and artifacts in candidate_manifest.json.
The agent runs candidates using the existing job.py rather than replacing it with autofl.yaml.
The agent records candidate results and reports the best reproducible candidate.

mandatory_behavior.description: runs deterministic job import before candidate edits
shows editable, unresolved, allowed, and fixed-budget campaign fields before running candidates
edits only files allowed by autofl.yaml
lets the coding agent implement source and algorithm candidates in an isolated draft
computes a manifest with base source, changed files, patch hash, budget hash, artifacts, and result
uses job.py and standard FLARE execution surfaces for candidate runs

prohibited_behavior.description: does not treat autofl.yaml as a replacement for job.py or an exported FLARE job
does not bypass startup-kit authentication, site policy, or normal job submission for production runs
does not edit outside the allowed edit paths
does not let built-in tunable suggestions replace agent candidate planning

process_metrics.description: whether deterministic import occurs before candidate code edits
number of candidate runs that changed fixed-budget fields without user approval
whether the requested metric is extracted from NVFLARE artifacts or logs
whether code candidates include deterministic source, budget, patch, and artifact provenance
number of production submissions or policy-sensitive actions performed without explicit user context
whether the final report includes commands, artifacts, changed files, and the best candidate

evals.prompt: My NVFLARE job in ./job.py is not getting very good accuracy. Please do a small local optimization pass - keep the data and evaluation setup as they are, and try two approaches you think are likely to help.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes the plain-language improvement request, invokes the NVFLARE Auto-FL skill, imports job.py into autofl.yaml, and runs a bounded two-candidate campaign through the official campaign runner while keeping the data and evaluation setup unchanged.
assertions: The selected skill is nvflare-autofl even though the prompt never mentions Auto-FL, campaigns, or candidates.
The agent runs all optimization through run_job_campaign.py rather than optimizing the project directly.
The agent limits the campaign to two evaluated candidates beyond the baseline, matching the requested number of approaches.
The agent keeps the data and evaluation setup unchanged while exploring candidates.
The agent reports the evaluated candidates and the best reproducible result when the campaign finishes.

mandatory_behavior.description: routes a plain-language low-accuracy or try-N-approaches request to the Auto-FL campaign without requiring Auto-FL terminology
runs deterministic job import before candidate edits
performs every optimization step through run_job_campaign.py
initializes the campaign with --max-candidates 2 and stops when the candidate cap is exhausted

prohibited_behavior.description: does not optimize or edit the user's project outside a prepared candidate
does not alter the data pipeline or evaluation setup while exploring candidates

evals.prompt: Continue my uncapped NVFLARE Auto-FL campaign on ./job.py. I just ran the literature pass you suggested and recorded the review with record --literature. The current .nvflare/autofl/campaign_state.json and results.tsv are attached. What do you run next?
evals.expected_output: The agent reads the code-owned campaign state, sees next_action=develop_literature_batch with required_exploration=source_backed_exploration for literature event lit-0001, and completes the remaining exploration batch — the ledger already holds the scored faithful implementation, so the agent prepares the tuned variant and the ablation with --family and --literature-event lit-0001 until 3 linked candidates have scored — before resuming argument-only tuning, treating the plateau clock as reset only when the batch completes.
assertions: The agent executes the state's next_action develop_literature_batch instead of choosing its own next step or resuming argument-only tuning.
The agent prepares each remaining batch candidate with prepare --family <slug> --literature-event lit-0001 so the linkage persists in the manifest and ledger.
The agent gives every literature-linked candidate source edits, because the runner rejects argument-only literature-linked candidates at evaluate time.

The agent brings the lit-0001 exploration batch to its required exploration_batch_size (3) scored source-backed candidates before normal candidate flow resumes; the faithful implementation is already scored in the ledger, so the tuned variant and the ablation remain.
The agent treats the plateau clock as reset when the final linked candidate scores, not when the literature review row was recorded.
The agent does not produce a final campaign answer while final_response_allowed is false.

mandatory_behavior.description: executes the code-owned next_action develop_literature_batch when the latest review's exploration batch is incomplete
prepares batch candidates with --family and --literature-event so they link to the recorded review in the manifest and ledger
completes exploration_batch_size scored source-backed candidates (faithful implementation, tuned variant, ablation) linked to the review before resuming normal candidate flow
treats the plateau clock as reset when the exploration batch completes, not when the review row is recorded

prohibited_behavior.description: does not link argument-only candidates to a literature event; the runner rejects them at evaluate time
does not resume argument-only tuning while state reports required_exploration=source_backed_exploration
does not claim the recorded review row alone relieved plateau pressure
does not produce a final campaign answer while final_response_allowed is false

process_metrics.description: whether exploration_batch_size scored source-backed candidates linked to lit-0001 complete before any argument-only tuning candidate runs
whether every batch candidate is prepared with --family and --literature-event and lands in the ledger with literature_event_id lit-0001
number of literature-linked candidates evaluated without source edits and rejected by the runner
number of times the agent claims plateau relief before the exploration batch completes
number of final campaign answers attempted while final_response_allowed is false

evals.prompt: Keep optimizing ./job.py with NVFLARE Auto-FL. My last six scored candidates were all argument-only FedAvg learning-rate, batch-size, and weight-decay tweaks, and the attached campaign state now reports diversify_candidates. What is the next candidate?
evals.expected_output: The agent honors next_action=diversify_candidates from the code-owned campaign state: instead of a seventh same-family argument-only attempt, it switches to a different algorithm family or prepares a source-backed candidate with a workload-appropriate idea, keeping the same campaign inputs, comparison budget, and ledger.
assertions: The agent recognizes the family_repeat_limit condition from the campaign state and the ledger's six consecutive argument-only fedavg attempts.
The agent's next candidate switches algorithm family or contains source edits rather than being another same-family argument-only tweak.
The agent selects workload-appropriate exploration such as client optimizer, loss, schedule, architecture, or server aggregation, not Byzantine-robust aggregation for a benign campaign.
The agent keeps the same job.py, autofl.yaml, metric, comparison budget, and ledger while diversifying.
The agent does not produce a final campaign answer while final_response_allowed is false.

mandatory_behavior.description: switches algorithm family or prepares a source-backed candidate when state reports diversify_candidates
treats family_repeat_limit consecutive same-family argument-only scored attempts as requiring a family switch or source-backed candidate
chooses exploration compatible with the workload's threat model and fixed comparison budget
keeps the same campaign inputs, metric, comparison budget, and ledger when diversifying

prohibited_behavior.description: does not respond to diversify_candidates with another argument-only candidate from the same algorithm family
does not select Byzantine-robust aggregation for a benign-client campaign
does not restart or replace the campaign to escape the diversification requirement

process_metrics.description: number of same-family argument-only candidates evaluated after state reported diversify_candidates
whether the first candidate after diversify_candidates switches family or contains source edits
number of candidates that conflict with the workload's threat model, such as Byzantine-robust aggregation in a benign campaign

evals.prompt: Convert this standalone PyTorch training script into an NVFLARE federated job.
evals.expected_output: The Auto-FL skill should not be the lead; a conversion skill should handle the request before Auto-FL is applicable.
assertions: The selected skill is nvflare-convert-pytorch, not nvflare-autofl.

evals.prompt: My NVFLARE job failed with EXECUTION_EXCEPTION. Diagnose the client logs and tell me what went wrong.
evals.expected_output: The Auto-FL skill should not be the lead; failed-job diagnosis should route to nvflare-diagnose-job.
assertions: The selected skill is nvflare-diagnose-job, not nvflare-autofl.

evals.prompt: Build a React landing page for a bakery.
assertions: The selected skill is none.
